choice-1.text: Wait to approach the mother until she starts laying eggs

choice-2.text: Follow the mother to start to her nesting site

choice-3.text: While she is laying the clutch

choice-4.text: Wait for her to finish and head back to the ocean

choice-5.text: Put all the eggs in bucket a bucket

choice-6.text: Put all the eggs in bucket and pack it tight with sand

choice-7.text: Run bucket to hatchery. We need to get them in the ground ASAP

choice-8.text: Walk bucket to hatchery. We don't want to jostle them

choice-9.text: Dig hole for the clutch about 15 inches deep

choice-10.text: Dig hole for the clutch about 30 inches deep

choice-11.text: Treat the sand with anti-fingal spray

choice-13.text: Lower the eggs into the nest with your hand

choice-14.text: Lower the eggs into the nest with a shovel.

choice-17.text: Cover the nest using your hands to scoop the sand

choice-18.text: Cover the nest using a shovel to scoop the sand

choice-19.text: Wait about 35 days. Then dig up the hatchlings.

choice-20.text: Wait for the hatchlings to come out on their own

choice-21.text: Collect the hatchlings into a bucket

choice-22.text: Open the hatchery door for the hactlings to find the oeacn

choice-23.text: Wait to release the hatchlings in the morning

choice-25.text: Build a ramp to guide the hatchlings to the oceam

choice-26.text: Let the hatchlings roam free to the ocean

choice-27.text: Release the hatchlings in the waves

choice-28.text: Release the hatchlings on the beach

choice-30.text: Most of the turtles are still alive!
